page: Microsoft Azure D32ns_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache, 128 GB of RAM providing 4 GB per core, and no bundled local storage, though the instance is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory bandwidth performance, with large-block read-only speeds reaching 210.27 GB/sec and write speeds at 92.5 GB/sec. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions), contribute to strong performance in specialized workloads. Single-threaded gzip compression reaches 31.42 GB/sec, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression achieves 196.62 GB/sec. In LLM inference tests, the server demonstrates top-tier text generation speeds of 364.66 tokens/sec for small 135M models and strong prompt processing speeds of 195.21 tokens/sec for medium 7B models, though performance drops to a weak 2.71 tokens/sec for large 70B models.

From a resource density perspective, the D32ns_v6 offers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring dedicated compute power and high network throughput without the added cost of bundled local storage. The lack of local storage requires external disk attachment, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This server is highly suited for network-intensive applications, static web serving, medium-scale database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure D32ns_v6 is a network-optimized, general-purpose virtual machine running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It is equipped with 32 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, paired with 128 GB of RAM and no local storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks show strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth, static web serving at 53.75 GB/sec, and small-to-medium LLM inference tasks. However, it exhibits weak performance on large 70B LLM text generation. Lacking local storage, it relies on external Premium Storage. This configuration is highly suitable for network-intensive applications, static web serving, and CPU-bound data processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32ns_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for high-throughput networking and general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32ns_v6 is a network-optimized VM with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and advanced AMX instruction support.
tagline: A dedicated Intel-powered virtual machine optimized for high network throughput, balanced memory allocation, and accelerated vector and matrix computations.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 4.2 GHz with 128 GB RAM
Network-optimized architecture with support for Premium Storage
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix math
Strong memory bandwidth and static web serving performance
Best suited for network-intensive applications, web serving, and medium CPU-based inference
